# HuggingFace Dataset Card Validation — `jam-actions-v0` (public subset)

**Generated by:** Slice 24 (publication dry-run, 2026-05-19)
**Source:** YAML frontmatter at the top of `README.md`
**Authority cross-checked against:** HuggingFace Hub dataset-cards docs (https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/datasets-cards) and the HF Datasets repository schema (https://github.com/huggingface/hub-docs/blob/main/datasetcard.md)

This is a **dry-run validation**. No `huggingface-cli upload` was executed; no HF API call was made. The validation methodology is manual schema-matching against the current public HF dataset-card spec.

## Current YAML frontmatter (verbatim)

```yaml
license: cc-by-sa-3.0
language:
  - en
pretty_name: "AI Jam Sessions — Tool-Use Traces v0 (Public Subset)"
size_categories:
  - n<1K
task_categories:
  - text-generation
  - other
task_ids: []
tags:
  - music
  - midi
  - mcp
  - tool-use
  - symbolic-music
  - piano
  - classical
configs:
  - config_name: default
    data_files:
      - split: train
        path: records.jsonl
```

## Field-by-field validation table

| Field | HF expectation | Current value | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `license` | One slug from HF's licensing enum; common slugs: `apache-2.0`, `mit`, `cc-by-4.0`, `cc-by-sa-3.0`, `cc-by-sa-4.0`, `other`, etc. | `cc-by-sa-3.0` | PASS | Slug is in HF's enum. Note: HF does NOT carry a `-de` jurisdiction variant; the DE jurisdiction is documented in `ATTRIBUTION.md` and `LICENSE-DATASET.md`. Obligations are identical to international 3.0. |
| `language` | List of ISO 639-1 codes | `["en"]` | PASS | English annotations only (KNOWN_LIMITATIONS section 5 confirms English-only). |
| `pretty_name` | Free-text display name (no slugification rule) | `"AI Jam Sessions — Tool-Use Traces v0 (Public Subset)"` | PASS | Short, human-readable; em-dash renders correctly in HF's title. |
| `size_categories` | List of enum slugs: `n<1K`, `1K<n<10K`, `10K<n<100K`, `100K<n<1M`, `1M<n<10M`, `n>10M` | `["n<1K"]` | PASS | 115 records is well under 1K; correct bucket. |
| `task_categories` | List of HF task-category slugs (e.g., `text-generation`, `text2text-generation`, `question-answering`, `summarization`, `tabular-classification`, `automatic-speech-recognition`, `other`, etc.) | `["text-generation", "other"]` | PASS | `text-generation` reflects that the model generates the tool-call trace + reasoning text. `other` is an honest fallback for the tool-use-over-symbolic-music aspect that HF's enum doesn't yet have a dedicated slug for. |
| `task_ids` | List of sub-task slugs (optional) | `[]` | PASS-WARN | Empty list is valid YAML, but HF would typically populate this if `task_categories` has an entry that has sub-tasks. For `text-generation` + `other`, no sub-task slug fits cleanly. Slice 24.5 doc-fix candidate: consider omitting the field entirely rather than declaring it empty (HF treats absent and empty the same, but absent is slightly cleaner). |
| `tags` | Free-form list | `["music", "midi", "mcp", "tool-use", "symbolic-music", "piano", "classical"]` | PASS | All tags are reasonable HF-side keywords. No slug constraints on this field. |
| `configs` | List of config objects with `config_name` + `data_files` (each item being a `{split, path}` pair); required when the dataset uses non-trivial config-based loading | One config named `default` with one split `train` pointing to `records.jsonl` | PASS-WARN | The split declaration is technically correct: `records.jsonl` carries every record with an embedded `split` field, so HF's `load_dataset` would default-load all 115 records as `train`. However, the test split (12 records of `clair-de-lune`) is not declared as a separate HF split. Consumer-side, the canonical held-out test is read from `splits.json` (carried in the package), not from HF's split mechanism. This is a deliberate choice — see "what an HF upload would do" below — but Slice 24.5 / 25 could optionally split the JSONL into `train.jsonl` + `test.jsonl` and declare both splits in `configs`. |
| `source_datasets` | List of upstream dataset slugs or human-readable labels (optional but recommended for derivative datasets) | NOT DECLARED | WARN | Missing. HF dataset-card spec lists this as recommended (not required). Slice 24.5 doc-fix candidate: add `source_datasets: ["original"]` (HF's enum value for "this dataset has an original upstream that is not itself on HF") — the upstream MIDI is piano-midi.de, which is not on HF. Alternative slugs: `extended` if HF treats this as an extension of an existing HF dataset (it does not). Recommend `["original"]`. |
| `multilinguality` | List of slugs from `["monolingual", "multilingual", "translation", "other"]` (optional) | NOT DECLARED | WARN | Missing. English-only annotations → `["monolingual"]`. Slice 24.5 doc-fix candidate. |
| `annotations_creators` | List of slugs from `["found", "crowdsourced", "expert-generated", "machine-generated", "other"]` (optional) | NOT DECLARED | WARN | Missing. Annotations are mostly model-generated (qwen2.5:7b) with human curation → `["machine-generated", "expert-generated"]`. Slice 24.5 doc-fix candidate. |
| `language_creators` | List of slugs from `["found", "crowdsourced", "expert-generated", "machine-generated", "other"]` (optional) | NOT DECLARED | WARN | Missing. Same population as `annotations_creators` for this dataset (the annotations are the only English-language layer). Slice 24.5 doc-fix candidate. |
| `pretty_description` | Short free-text description (optional, distinct from the body's "Dataset Summary") | NOT DECLARED | WARN | Missing. This field is rarely used and can be derived from `pretty_name` + the body summary. Not a blocker. |

## Summary

**Validation result: PASS for actual HF upload, with 5 WARN-level gaps that are doc-fix candidates for Slice 24.5 / 25.**

- All HF-required fields (`license`, `task_categories`, `language`, `configs`) are populated and schema-valid.
- 0 FAIL items. The card would be accepted by HF on upload.
- 2 PASS-WARN items (`task_ids` and `configs` test-split nuance) — both deliberate; both could be polished in a docs-only follow-up.
- 5 WARN items (`source_datasets`, `multilinguality`, `annotations_creators`, `language_creators`, `pretty_description`) — all optional in the current HF schema; populating them improves discoverability and metadata richness but does not block publication.

No FAIL items. The dataset card is publication-ready for HuggingFace as-is. The WARN items are quality polish, not blockers.

## What an HF upload would do (walkthrough)

When the operator decides to publish to HF (Slice 25 or later), the upload mechanics are:

1. **Create the HF dataset repo.** Either via the web UI (https://huggingface.co/new-dataset) under the `mcp-tool-shop-org` HF organization, or via `huggingface-cli repo create mcp-tool-shop-org/jam-actions-v0 --type dataset` if the org exists on HF.
2. **Upload the package contents.** Two paths:
   - `huggingface-cli upload mcp-tool-shop-org/jam-actions-v0 ./datasets/jam-actions-v0-public/ . --repo-type dataset --commit-message "v0.4.2 — initial public release"` (single command, recursive)
   - `huggingface_hub.HfApi().upload_folder(...)` from Python (programmatic, can attach finer-grained metadata)
3. **HF reads `README.md` as the dataset card.** The YAML frontmatter validated above drives:
   - The license badge ("CC-BY-SA-3.0" rendered on the card header)
   - The discoverability search facets (`task_categories`, `language`, `size_categories`, `tags`)
   - The auto-generated dataset preview (HF parses `configs` and offers `load_dataset("mcp-tool-shop-org/jam-actions-v0")` against the declared splits)
4. **HF reads `records.jsonl` as the canonical data file.** Per the `configs.default.data_files[0]` declaration, `load_dataset("mcp-tool-shop-org/jam-actions-v0")` returns a `DatasetDict` with one split (`train`) of 115 rows. Consumers who want the canonical held-out test set must read `splits.json` from the same repo (downloadable as a sidecar file via `huggingface_hub.hf_hub_download`).
5. **Sidecar files become repo-attachments.** Every other file in the package (CITATION.cff, ATTRIBUTION.md, LICENSE-DATASET.md, KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md, DATASET_SCHEMA.md, RELEASE_NOTES.md, manifest.json, splits.json, provenance-verification.json, zenodo-metadata.json, hf-dataset-card-check.md, evals/*, records/*, pianoroll/*, checksums.sha256, VERSION, package-inputs.json) is accessible at `https://huggingface.co/datasets/mcp-tool-shop-org/jam-actions-v0/blob/main/<path>`. Standard `huggingface_hub.hf_hub_download(repo_id, filename, repo_type="dataset")` retrieves any of them.
6. **Eval artifacts become discoverable.** The `evals/` directory contains the canonical Slice 22 RC-gate PASS verdict (`slice22-release-gate-revised-assessment.json`); HF doesn't auto-render eval JSON, but the file is readable via the repo browser and via `hf_hub_download`.

## Gaps that would block actual publication

**None at the schema level.** All HF-required fields are present and valid; all WARN-level items are optional in the current spec.

The gating items for actual publication are operator-level decisions, not schema gaps:

- **HF organization choice.** `mcp-tool-shop-org` does not yet have an HF presence; an HF org needs to be created (or the dataset uploaded under a personal namespace first).
- **Auth tokens.** An HF write token is required to upload. Per Slice 24's hard rule, NO auth tokens are in scope; this is a Slice 25 (actual publish) precondition.
- **Provenance manifest cross-check.** The `provenance-verification.json` references `http://piano-midi.de/` (HTTP only — no HTTPS endpoint). HF's CSP does not block this in the README body, but downstream consumers behind strict CSP will see broken upstream links. Documenting this is in `ATTRIBUTION.md` already; no fix needed for HF upload.

## Cross-link to canonical PASS artifact

The canonical Slice 22 RC-gate PASS verdict is at `evals/slice22-release-gate-revised-assessment.json` within this package. After HF upload, it is accessible at:

`https://huggingface.co/datasets/<owner>/<repo>/resolve/main/evals/slice22-release-gate-revised-assessment.json`

The verdict is `aggregate: "PASS"` with all 6 blocking axes (axes 1–6) clearing per the `release-gate-assessment/2.0.0` schema. This artifact is the canonical reference for the dataset's gate-cleared state.

## Slice 24.5 / 25 follow-up checklist (optional polish)

If the operator wants to address the 5 WARN-level gaps before actual upload:

- [ ] Add `source_datasets: ["original"]` to YAML frontmatter
- [ ] Add `multilinguality: ["monolingual"]` to YAML frontmatter
- [ ] Add `annotations_creators: ["machine-generated", "expert-generated"]` to YAML frontmatter
- [ ] Add `language_creators: ["machine-generated", "expert-generated"]` to YAML frontmatter
- [ ] Optionally remove the empty `task_ids: []` declaration
- [ ] Optionally split `records.jsonl` into `train.jsonl` + `test.jsonl` and update `configs` to declare both splits explicitly (this requires re-running the packager; may bump version to 0.5.0)

None of these are required for a successful HF upload. They are quality-polish items that improve discoverability and metadata richness.
